measurement_note: Size is W, the number of words. The 64/256/640 tiers span 10x and repeat descending word lengths 20 through 1 while remaining below the 100000-character limit. Stable key sorting takes O(N + W log W), and stable length buckets provide an independent O(N)-time check. A correct stable insertion sort repeatedly shifts earlier longer words, takes O(W^2) on these inputs, completes normally at W = 640, and is rejected by scaling.
